Also why is there a 2-hour delay if I use cold water on the normal cycle... That's just crazy. I even bought an extended warranty and for some reason I'm having trouble getting anybody to hear me out about my washing machine. If I spend over $300 on an item I expect it to work on all settings. Also this product would be way more maneuverable if I had multiple wheels on all four sides instead of just two in the back You almost have to tip the machine completely on its side to get it to roll. And the handles are so low on the machine I'm not sure exactly how they're supposed to be worked. Super disappointed in Black & Decker also for not backing up your products. So in conclusion if you would like a washer that works halfway this is the one for you! However if you would like your hard earned money to do what it's supposed to do look elsewhere! I will be updating my review if anyone reaches out to me or I get the problem solved!

product_title: Keurig My K-Cup Reusable Coffee Filter - Old Model
clean_review_text: Part of the reason for our Keurig purchase was this item. We could get the reusable filter and not have to go through the waste of the k-cups. Sadly, this makes a sub-par cup of coffee. Not only is it tedious, you have to go through all kinds of extra steps that aren't part of the manufacturer's intended instructions to get it to make a decent cup of coffee. Most coffee comes out weak unless you put a used k-cup at the bottom of the thing before putting in the filter to slow the flow of the water. Plus, you gave to pack in a LOT of coffee to get a reasonable cup of coffee. Then, so it doesn't overflow and put grounds in your cup, I use a toothpick to kind of bore out the center so the water can flow in a little better. You can stop the leakage for the most part by putting a rubber band around the very top where the lid screws on, but if I'm paying as much as $20 for this, then I'd like it to work without my having to put in so much effort. I've also read several reviews about how quickly these break, and while mine has not yet broken, I can see where it will. Very shoddy construction. It's a disappointment. To sum it up, you CAN get a good cup of coffee out of this, but it's going to take work and practice to figure it out. Nothing like just plopping in a new k-cup and closing the cover and pushing the button...
